expectations.text: Runs Step 0 diagnostic checks before starting setup
expectations.evidence: Step 0 runs 5 checks (node_modules, .env, ports 3000/5173, pnpm vs npm, node version) before any setup actions
Check 5: 'Tool call: Bash with command node --version', expected result 'v20.11.0'

expectations.text: Detects npm vs pnpm mismatch and explains why pnpm is needed
expectations.evidence: Diagnostic summary: 'this project is a pnpm monorepo, so it needs pnpm install rather than npm install -- npm doesn't wire up the workspace dependencies between the backend and frontend packages correctly'
Step 1: 'Tool call: Bash with command cd ... && pnpm install'
Step 2: 'Tool call: Bash with command cp .env.example .env'

expectations.text: Asks user for their Anthropic API key
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'I need your Anthropic API key to configure the project. You can get one from https://console.anthropic.com/settings/keys'

expectations.text: Does not echo API key to terminal output
expectations.evidence: Step 2: 'The skill specifically says: Do NOT echo the key to the terminal... So I use the Edit tool rather than a shell command like sed or echo'
Step 3: 'Tool call: Bash with command pnpm --filter @new-workshop/agent-service dev, with run_in_background: true'
Step 4: 'Tool call: Bash with command pnpm --filter @new-workshop/web-client dev, with run_in_background: true'

expectations.text: Verifies frontend is running by curling localhost:5173 (not just port check)
expectations.evidence: Step 4: 'Tool call: Bash with command curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}" http://localhost:5173', result '200'

expectations.text: Presents both localhost:3000 and localhost:5173 URLs
Mentions available agent personas from agents/ directory after setup
expectations.evidence: Bonus: 'You have 2 agent persona(s) defined in agents/. Try chatting with one in the UI!'

expectations.text: Follows correct order: diagnostic -> install -> env config -> start services
expectations.evidence: Order: Step 0 (diagnostic) -> Step 1 (pnpm install) -> Step 2 (env config) -> Steps 3-4 (start services) -> Step 5 (result)

expectations.text: Acknowledges user already attempted install, doesn't ignore their context
expectations.evidence: Diagnostic summary explicitly addresses user's npm install: 'I can see two things going on here. First, this project is a pnpm monorepo, so it needs pnpm install rather than npm install... No worries, that's an easy fix. Second, you're missing a .env file... that's why you're seeing the API key error.'
